questions.question: The standard musical staff has how many lines?
questions.explanation: The musical staff consists of 5 horizontal lines.

questions.question: The treble clef is also known as the
questions.explanation: The treble clef is the G clef.

questions.question: How many semitones are in an octave?
The time signature 4/4 means
options: 4 beats per measure, quarter note gets one beat
3 beats per measure, quarter note gets one beat
2 beats per measure, half note gets one beat
8 beats per measure, eighth note gets one beat
questions.explanation: 4/4 time has 4 beats per measure with a quarter note receiving one beat.

questions.question: Which instrument is not a percussion instrument?
questions.explanation: Violin is a string instrument, not percussion.

questions.question: The musical note with the shortest duration is
questions.explanation: The sixteenth note has the shortest standard duration.

questions.question: A major scale has how many semitones between the tonic and octave?
questions.explanation: A major scale spans 12 semitones (one octave).

questions.question: The term 'forte' in music means
questions.explanation: Forte means loud or strong in Italian musical terms.

questions.explanation: Tempo refers to the speed of music.
